title: G.E. Breaks Up With Its Storied Past
content: The company, once the epitome of corporate ambition, plans to split itself into three separate companies.

more_info.byline: By Steve Lohr and Michael J. de la Merced

en_title: G.E. Breaks Up With Its Storied Past
en_content: The company, once the epitome of corporate ambition, plans to split itself into three separate companies.

title: Moderna and U.S. at Odds Over Vaccine Patent Rights
content: Moderna’s patent application names several employees as the sole inventors of a crucial component of its coronavirus vaccine, excluding three government scientists.

more_info.byline: By Sheryl Gay Stolberg and Rebecca Robbins

en_title: Moderna and U.S. at Odds Over Vaccine Patent Rights
en_content: Moderna’s patent application names several employees as the sole inventors of a crucial component of its coronavirus vaccine, excluding three government scientists.

title: The Biggest Kink in America’s Supply Chain: Not Enough Truckers
content: Long hours and uncomfortable working conditions are leading to a shortage of truck drivers, which has compounded shipping delays in the United States.

more_info.byline: By Madeleine Ngo and Ana Swanson

en_title: The Biggest Kink in America’s Supply Chain: Not Enough Truckers
en_content: Long hours and uncomfortable working conditions are leading to a shortage of truck drivers, which has compounded shipping delays in the United States.

title: Meta plans to remove thousands of sensitive ad-targeting categories.
content: Ad buyers will no longer be able to use topics such as health, race or sexual orientation to target people with unwanted ads on Facebook or other Meta apps.

more_info.byline: By Mike Isaac and Tiffany Hsu

en_title: Meta plans to remove thousands of sensitive ad-targeting categories.
en_content: Ad buyers will no longer be able to use topics such as health, race or sexual orientation to target people with unwanted ads on Facebook or other Meta apps.

title: How Data Is Reshaping Real Estate
content: Tech start-ups are offering new tools to help retailers and entertainment venues be more efficient by counting crowds, tracking foot traffic and following local shopping habits.
en_title: How Data Is Reshaping Real Estate
en_content: Tech start-ups are offering new tools to help retailers and entertainment venues be more efficient by counting crowds, tracking foot traffic and following local shopping habits.

title: Rolls-Royce plans to build small nuclear power plants in Britain.
content: Backed by the government and other investors, the industrial giant proposes to build as many as 16 generating plants.
en_title: Rolls-Royce plans to build small nuclear power plants in Britain.
en_content: Backed by the government and other investors, the industrial giant proposes to build as many as 16 generating plants.

title: As China’s Property Crisis Spreads, Beijing Says There’s Nothing to See
content: Global markets just weeks ago were fretting over the possible failure of Evergrande. Now the developer says the worst is over, even as other companies show signs of trouble.

more_info.byline: By Alexandra Stevenson and Joy Dong

en_title: As China’s Property Crisis Spreads, Beijing Says There’s Nothing to See
en_content: Global markets just weeks ago were fretting over the possible failure of Evergrande. Now the developer says the worst is over, even as other companies show signs of trouble.

title: Winter Heating Bills Loom as the Next Inflation Threat
content: After plunging during the pandemic as the economy slowed, energy prices have roared upward. Washington is already moving to offer homeowners relief.
en_title: Winter Heating Bills Loom as the Next Inflation Threat
en_content: After plunging during the pandemic as the economy slowed, energy prices have roared upward. Washington is already moving to offer homeowners relief.

title: Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, says paid family leave ‘is not just about the mom.’
content: “It sets us up for economic growth and success, but it also just allows people to have that very sacred time as a family.”
en_title: Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, says paid family leave ‘is not just about the mom.’
en_content: “It sets us up for economic growth and success, but it also just allows people to have that very sacred time as a family.”

title: Oklahoma Supreme Court Throws Out $465 Million Opioid Ruling Against J.&J.
content: The 5-1 decision is a setback for those who are fighting to hold big drug makers accountable for the opioid epidemic.

en_content: The 5-1 decision is a setback for those who are fighting to hold big drug makers accountable for the opioid epidemic.

title: Workhorse Group records a big loss on electric vans.

title: Starbucks workers at three more Buffalo-area stores file for union elections.

title: Powell’s Books Survived Amazon. Can It Reinvent Itself After the Pandemic?
content: As much as any city, Portland, Ore., has been through hell. Its landmark store, Powell’s Books, must finally build a viable online business while recapturing its downtown success.

title: Starbucks Seeks to Delay Union Election as Vote Nears
content: The move, and a visit by the company’s former chief executive days before the scheduled start of balloting at three Buffalo-area stores, reflect the stakes.

title: With Tears, Hugs and Balloons, U.S. Allows Vaccinated Foreign Travelers to Enter

title: Wirecutter workers threaten a walkout during a peak shopping week over contract delays.

title: Randal Quarles, formerly the Fed’s vice chair for supervision, will leave the central bank at the end of December.
content: Mr. Quarles’s term as a governor could have stretched until 2032. His departure will give the Biden administration more room to remake the Fed.
en_title: Randal Quarles, formerly the Fed’s vice chair for supervision, will leave the central bank at the end of December.
en_content: Mr. Quarles’s term as a governor could have stretched until 2032. His departure will give the Biden administration more room to remake the Fed.

title: Behind Elon Musk’s Twitter Poll Is a Tax Bill Coming Due
content: He asked whether he should sell 10 percent of his stock, and Twitter voters said yes. Stock options he was granted in 2012 are “a ticking tax time bomb,” one expert said.
en_title: Behind Elon Musk’s Twitter Poll Is a Tax Bill Coming Due
